title: ugetbind
text: ugetbind Get the internal bind name of a Uniface field. Explain this code int ugetbind (unsigned char*fieldname,unsigned char*bindname); Copy Parameters fieldname Name of a field in your component. To resolve ambiguity, you can qualify the field name with an entity name, for example, fieldname . entity . bindname String that receives the internal bind name. This buffer should be at least 6 bytes in length. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ugetbind Value Meaning 0 The bind name was successfully retrieved 1 Field is not a valid field name. It might be misspelled or ambiguous. Use Library: yrtl Description ugetbind returns the so-called ‘internal bind name’ of the specified field. This is a string consisting of one to five digits, so your buffer needs to be 6 bytes in length. Using the internal bind name is never mandatory, but it is more efficient than using the field name when you call ufget , ufgetc , ufput , ufputi , and ufputc repeatedly to get information about the same field. See the description of ufput for example code using ugetbind . Example: ufput, ufputc, ufputi, and ugetbind The following example reads a graphics file from disk in chunks of 5000 bytes, and transfers the contents to the Uniface image field IMGFIELD. This field should be defined in the form as: Data Type—Image (database) Widget Type—Picture Interface—R* Note that, in the calls to ufput and ufputc , we specify a negative length, because we are not sure if there is more data until we have read the entire file. Once we have read all the file, we issue a final call to ufputc with length zero to denote that the transfer is complete. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> EXPORT void GETIMAGE () { FILE* fp; short len; char buf[5000]; char bindname[80]; /* Use bindname for efficiency */ ugetbind("IMGFIELD", bindname); /* Open binary read mode */ fp = fopen("d:\\temp\\mypicture.jpg", "rb"); len = fread(buf, 1, 5000, fp); /* Read first chunk */ ufput(bindname, buf, -len); /* Assume more to come */ while (len > 0) { len = fread(buf, 1, 5000, fp); /* Read next chunk */ if ( len > 0 ) /* Assume more to come */ ufputc(bindname, buf, -len); } ufputc(bindname, buf, 0); /* Tell Uniface it's done */ fclose(fp); /* and close file */ } Copy Related Links ufget Get data from a field displayed in a Uniface component. ufgetc Get additional data from a field displayed in a Uniface component. ufput Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ufputc Copy additional data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. ufputi Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field as if the switch /init were used.
